provenance.source_corpus: Divergent Pathways of Successional Recovery for In Situ Oil Sands Exploration Drilling Pads on Wooded Moderate‐Rich Fens in Alberta, Canada

source_spans.text: OSE pad preparation modifies banks of vegetative propagules and seeds, exposes lower layers of non-decomposed peat, and reduces variation in microtopography and microhabitats available for bryophyte and vascular plant species.

OSE pad preparation modifies banks of vegetative propagules and seeds, exposes lower layers of non-decomposed peat, and reduces variation in microtopography and microhabitats available for bryophyte and vascular plant species.

Loss of vertical microtopography and prolonged surface flooding after OSE operations were associated with major shifts in vegetation composition away from reference habitat conditions and will need to be managed.
Flooding has also been shown to affect the establishment of some moss species negatively (Granath et al. 2010)
As flooding in summer is detrimental to most boreal forest trees (Grossnickle 2000), tree seedlings are unlikely to survive in the bryophyte-poor OSE habitat.

Loss of vertical microtopography and prolonged surface flooding after OSE operations were associated with major shifts in vegetation composition away from reference habitat conditions and will need to be managed.

nodes.name: Removal of extant vegetation by blading

source_spans.text: Removal of extant vegetation by blading prior to drilling in winter should be minimized to increase fen surface height and to retain plant reproductive fragments and diaspores for regeneration.
Removal of extant vegetation by blading prior to drilling in winter should be minimized to increase fen surface height and to retain plant reproductive fragments and diaspores for regeneration.
Removal of extant vegetation by blading prior to drilling in winter should be minimized to increase fen surface height and to retain plant reproductive fragments and diaspores for regeneration.

nodes.name: Removing large blocks of frozen peat

source_spans.text: Removing large blocks of frozen peat during pad abandonment in winter and dispersing these on-site could be a simple, cost-effective technique, warranting investigation.
These could create microsites that are elevated above the water table surface during high water levels, for colonization of woody plants and hummock-building mosses
Once established, Sphagnum can influence the establishment of other species (Malmer et al. 2003)
Once established, Sphagnum can influence the establishment of other species (Malmer et al. 2003)

The moss Polytrichum strictum was a strong indicator of bryophyte-dominated as compared to bryophyte-poor OSE habitat and may facilitate establishment of Sphagnum and other plant species at those sites (Groeneveld et al. 2007).

The moss Polytrichum strictum was a strong indicator of bryophyte-dominated as compared to bryophyte-poor OSE habitat and may facilitate establishment of Sphagnum and other plant species at those sites (Groeneveld et al. 2007).
However, high substrate surface temperatures in bryophyte-poor OSE habitat (R. Caners, unpublished data) could potentially accelerate residual peat decomposition (Karofeld et al. 2008)
However, high substrate surface temperatures in bryophyte-poor OSE habitat (R. Caners, unpublished data) could potentially accelerate residual peat decomposition (Karofeld et al. 2008)
Sedges will probably dominate the disturbed OSE habitats until the sedge peat accumulates sufficiently to develop elevated microsites.
Sedges will probably dominate the disturbed OSE habitats until the sedge peat accumulates sufficiently to develop elevated microsites.
